rationale: The citation is used to justify a core assumption in the citing paper: consumers often do not spontaneously convert miles and dollars into a common unit when evaluating prices. The citing paper also revisits this source to address an alternative relativistic-processing explanation and again in the conclusion when explaining why combined-currency pricing can work. This repeated conceptual and interpretive use goes beyond a passing background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Nunes and Park (2003) as key conceptual support for non-conversion across currencies, which underpins its formal model of combined-currency pricing. It also uses that prior finding to interpret results and reject an alternative account of observed choices.
